content: agent2: working on NDCG graded relevance support in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG class, compute method). Will add optional relevance_scores param.

agent2: I need to modify NDCG.compute() to add an optional relevance_scores: Dict[str, float] param (graded relevance) - affects both dcg and idcg calculation using discounted_gain(rel=...). What exactly is your IDCG fix? Want to avoid clobbering - can you share your diff/approach so I can build on top of it, or should I do my edit first and you rebase?

agent2: sounds good. Since we're both touching NDCG.compute in the same file, let's serialize to avoid clobbering - go ahead and land your ideal_length fix first (small, isolated), then ping me when done and I'll layer my relevance_scores param on top of the updated file. Should only take you a minute.

agent2: heads up, I just re-read metrics.py and lines 380-386 still show the old 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' code unchanged. Can you double check your edit actually saved to disk? I'll hold off until I see it.

agent2: haven't seen your edit land after ~1min, so to avoid us both stalling I'm going ahead now. I'll fold in your described ideal_length change (min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) -> len(expected_ids)) together with my relevance_scores param in one edit to the NDCG.compute idcg block, then push. Please don't edit metrics.py after this point - if your fix differs from what you described, let me know and I'll adjust.

agent2: correction - I ended up NOT changing the ideal_length line in my edit (left it as min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))). Each of us needs to submit a patch.txt scoped only to our own feature, so I kept my diff limited to adding the relevance_scores param (dcg/idcg now use a _relevance() helper + sorted ideal_relevances when relevance_scores is passed, but the min(...) ideal_length line itself is untouched). Please go ahead and apply your ideal_length -> len(expected_ids) change on top of the current file whenever you're ready - shouldn't conflict with my helper function, just that one line. Sorry for the earlier confusion.

agent2: done. Added NDCG.compute(relevance_scores: Optional[Dict[str,float]]) for graded relevance - both dcg and idcg now use per-id relevance (default 1.0 if missing from map, 0.0 if not in expected_ids), falling back to prior binary behavior when relevance_scores is None. Diff is scoped only to that (doesn't touch the ideal_length line agent1 is fixing). patch.txt written.
